query: I added safe access everywhere to stop my widget crashing: {volume?.level}. But volume is just a number (my defpoll returns the integer percent like 42), and the widget still errors. I thought ?. made any access safe. What gives?
expected_behavior: Routes to the Safe Access ?. section (SKILL.md 'Safe Access' / 'Common Mistakes' and OPERATORS.md 'When safe access is NOT enough')
States the wall: ?. only guards against the left side being empty string or JSON null — it does NOT protect against type mismatches; indexing a number or plain string with ?.field still errors
Explains volume is a number, so .level (or ?.level) is meaningless — there is no field to access on a scalar
Gives the right fix: drop the field access (use {volume} directly) or use elvis {volume ?: "0"} to guard a possibly-empty value, not safe field access
Clarifies ?. is for optional nested fields on JSON objects/arrays from defpoll/deflisten that may not have fired yet
notes: Guards against the misconception that ?. is a blanket try/catch. eww safe access only short-circuits on null/empty — it does not catch 'cannot index a number/string'. Sprinkling ?. on scalar values gives false confidence and the widget still errors. The skill calls this out under 'When safe access is NOT enough' and Common Mistake 5.
